title: Nesogenes glandulosus
taxonomy: Nesogenes glandulosus is classified as a species within the family Bignoniaceae. This family is commonly known as the Bignonia family or the trumpet creepers. Nesogenes glandulosus is native to the tropical regions of Central America, particularly in countries such as Costa Rica and Panama. It belongs to the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants that includes numerous species of vines, shrubs, and trees. The classification of Nesogenes glandulosus further underscores its unique characteristics and relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Nesogenes glandulosus, also known as the "glandular Nesogenes," is a plant species belonging to the family Gesneriaceae, characterized by its unique, glandular hairs that secrete a fragrant, resinous substance. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections.
